text.title: competitive-positioning Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for competitive-positioning as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent regenerating the canvas without checking whether the in-flight backlog can deliver the new positioning.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Canvas-first, backlog-second sequencing creates a positioning that the current development capacity cannot deliver; every regen prompt must include the in-flight roadmap.

failure-mode.description: Agent widening the canvas to absorb every lost-deal feature request (sales-laundered positioning).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Feature-list widening destroys differentiation; pre-process win/loss notes to extract the category of competitor, not feature checklists, and reject feature-list dilution at the prompt level.

failure-mode.description: Agent generating positioning sections with "customer-centric approach," "best-in-class," or "innovative" as unique attributes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: These terms are claims every competitor makes; require a specific named attribute from the canvas and a citation that differentiates it from direct competitors.

failure-mode.description: PRD positioning section auto-filled with generic text like "this reinforces our customer-centric approach" without naming a specific canvas attribute.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Generic positioning answers are worse than no answer; they pass the gate-loop check superficially while adding no information, and they train reviewers to stop reading the positioning section.
